rationale: In the introduction and general discussion, this citation is used to identify a specific prior stream showing that arousing content increases sharing (social transmission). The authors explicitly position their work as complementary to Berger and Milkman (2012), shifting attention from recipient sharing motivation to how expressed arousal in reviews shapes helpfulness judgments. This role is central to the paper's framing of its contribution, not just a passing literature-list mention.
relation_description: The citing paper builds on Berger and Milkman (2012) as prior evidence that arousal drives online transmission. It extends that line by examining a different outcome (perceived review helpfulness) and a different process focus (reader inferences about reviewer effort from expressed arousal cues).
